title: sideritis hirsuta subsp. tomentosa
intro: Sideritis hirsuta subsp. tomentosa, also known as Greek mountain tea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and providing relief from respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. Sideritis hirsuta subsp. tomentosa is commonly consumed as a tea, either on its own or blended with other herbs, and can also be found in capsule or tincture form for easier consumption.
